# Snapie Auth — LLM Integration Reference
# Version: 1.3 | Updated: 2026-06-08
# Base URL: https://auth.snapie.io/api

Snapie Auth is a Hive blockchain account gateway and signing proxy.
It authenticates users via Google OAuth or email/password, creates
custodial Hive accounts, and signs Hive operations on behalf of users.

## Authentication Model

All user-facing endpoints require a session cookie (`snapieauth_session`)
set by the server after login. State-mutating endpoints also require the
`x-csrf-token` header, read from the JS-accessible `snapieauth_csrf` cookie.

There is NO Bearer token for user APIs. The Internal API (service-to-service)
uses `Authorization: Bearer <INTERNAL_API_KEY>`.

Always include `credentials: 'include'` in fetch calls.

## Key Concepts

CUSTODIAL: Snapie generates all 4 Hive keypairs server-side (owner, active,
posting, memo). Keys are AES-256-GCM encrypted with a server-derived key.
The user never sees or holds keys until voluntary emancipation.

EMANCIPATED: User holds their own keys. Snapie only has posting authority
(granted by the user). Active-level ops return `{ needsClientSigning: true }`.

SPONSOR TOKEN: A one-time token tied to an email hash. When redeemed, it
bypasses the daily free-account quota (global + per-IP) and the
"previously had account" restriction. The account still consumes one ACT.
Issued by admins via /admin.html or the Admin API, or by external apps
via the Internal API.

EMANCIPATION THRESHOLD: Configurable USD value. When a custodial account
exceeds this value, the user must export their keys before continuing.
Set EMANCIPATION_THRESHOLD_USD=0 to disable.

FREE ACCOUNT QUOTA: By default 10 accounts/day globally and 2/IP/day.
Check remaining slots with GET /quota (public, no auth). Sponsor tokens
bypass this limit entirely.

ACCOUNT CREATION FLOW:
1. Check GET /quota — if remaining > 0, user can proceed (or if sponsored)
2. POST /account/create → returns jobId
3. Poll GET /account/job/:jobId every 3-5 seconds
4. Status: pending → broadcasting → acked → confirmed (or failed/expired)
5. On confirmed: user.hiveUsername is set

## Quick Start Integration

```javascript
// 1. Check quota before sending users to register
const quota = await fetch('/api/quota').then(r => r.json())
// { total: 10, used: 3, remaining: 7, resetsAt: "2026-06-09T00:00:00.000Z" }
if (quota.remaining === 0) {
  // show "no slots today — request a sponsor invite or try tomorrow"
}

// 2. Google login — returns { user: SnapieUser } directly, no extra /auth/me needed
async function loginWithGoogle(idToken) {
  const res = await fetch('/api/auth/google', {
    method: 'POST',
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ credential: idToken })
  })
  const { user } = await res.json()
  // user.hiveUsername is null until account is created
  return user
}

// Email login works the same way
async function loginWithEmail(email, password) {
  const res = await fetch('/api/auth/email/login', {
    method: 'POST',
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ email, password })
  })
  if (res.status === 403) throw new Error('email_not_verified')
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error((await res.json()).error)
  const { user } = await res.json()
  return user
}

// Call GET /auth/me only when you need email or account value info,
// or to re-validate an existing session on page load.
async function getMe() {
  const res = await fetch('/api/auth/me', { credentials: 'include' })
  if (!res.ok) return null
  return (await res.json()).user
}

// 3. Read CSRF token
function getCsrf() {
  const m = document.cookie.match(/snapieauth_csrf=([^;]+)/)
  return m ? decodeURIComponent(m[1]) : null
}

// 4. Authenticated API call
async function api(method, path, body) {
  const res = await fetch(`/api${path}`, {
    method,
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      'x-csrf-token': getCsrf()
    },
    body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined
  })
  return res.json()
}

// 5. Create a custodial Hive account
const { jobId } = await api('POST', '/account/create', {
  username: 'alice',
  custodyMode: 'custodial'
})

// 6. Poll until confirmed
let job
do {
  await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 3000))
  job = await api('GET', `/account/job/${jobId}`)
} while (!['confirmed', 'failed', 'expired'].includes(job.status))
```

## Internal API — Service-to-Service

All /api/internal/* endpoints require:
  Authorization: Bearer <INTERNAL_API_KEY>

### Provision an account (e.g., to sell/gift accounts)
POST /api/internal/provision-account
Body: { username, custodyMode, linkedEmail?, returnKeysOnce?, note? }
Returns: { jobId, userId }

Poll: GET /api/internal/jobs/:jobId
If returnKeysOnce=true and status=confirmed: returns keys exactly once, then wipes.

### Issue a sponsor token (e.g., reward a user in an external app)
POST /api/internal/issue-sponsor-token
Body: { email, note?, expiresInDays? }
Returns: { tokenId, email, expiresAt }

The token is redeemed automatically when the user registers with that email
and calls POST /api/account/create. They bypass the daily quota and the
"previously had account" restriction — but one ACT is still consumed.

## SnapieUser Object

Login endpoints (POST /auth/google, POST /auth/email/login) return this directly
in the response body — no separate GET /auth/me call needed after login:

```
{
  id:           string   — MongoDB ObjectId as hex string
  name:         string|null
  picture:      string|null  — URL (Google accounts only)
  hiveUsername: string|null  — null until account is created
  custodyMode:  "custodial"|"emancipated"|null
  isAdmin:      boolean
}
```

GET /auth/me returns the same shape plus:
  email:                string|null  — from session JWT (cosmetic, not stored)
  accountValueUsd:      number|null  — only present when hiveUsername + custodial
  emancipationRequired: boolean      — only present when above applies

Note: after a fresh login, hiveUsername is null until the user creates a Hive
account via POST /account/create. Always check hiveUsername before using it.

## Endpoints Summary

### Public (no auth)
GET  /health                    → { ok: true }
GET  /quota                     → { total, used, remaining, resetsAt }
GET  /public-config             → { googleClientId, hiveNetwork }
POST /auth/google               → session cookies set + { user: SnapieUser }
POST /auth/email/register       → 202 { pending: true } — sends verification email
POST /auth/email/login          → session cookies set + { user: SnapieUser }
POST /auth/email/resend         → 200 { ok: true }
GET  /auth/email/verify         → redirect to /manage.html (email verification link)

### Session required (cookie)
GET  /auth/me                   → { user: SnapieUser + email + accountValueUsd? + emancipationRequired? }
POST /auth/logout               → clears cookies

GET  /account/eligibility       → { canCreate, canLinkExisting, reason, sponsored? }
GET  /account/check-username/:u → { available, reason? }
POST /account/create            → { jobId, sponsored }   [CSRF]
GET  /account/job/:jobId        → { status, txId, error }
GET  /account/value             → { hive, hbd, hp, totalUsd, emancipationRequired }

POST /link/verify-challenge     → { challenge }          [CSRF]
POST /link/confirm              → { ok, hiveUsername }   [CSRF]

POST /hive/sign-message         → { signature, account } or { needsClientSigning, message, account }  [CSRF]
  Body: { message: string (max 512 chars) }
  Custodial: server signs with the user's posting key and returns the signature.
  Emancipated: returns needsClientSigning=true — client must sign via Keychain/AIOha.
  Use for: challenge-response identity verification, feature access gates, chat auth.

POST /hive/broadcast            → { txId } or { needsClientSigning, unsignedOp }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/transfer             → same                   [CSRF]
POST /hive/power-up             → same                   [CSRF]
POST /hive/power-down           → same                   [CSRF]
POST /hive/delegate             → same                   [CSRF]
POST /hive/claim-rewards        → { txId }               [CSRF]

GET  /emancipate/status         → { custodyMode, totalUsd, thresholdUsd }
POST /emancipate/start          → { keys: { owner, active, posting, memo } } [CSRF]

### Internal (Bearer token)
POST /internal/provision-account      → { jobId, userId }
GET  /internal/jobs/:jobId            → { status, ...keys? }
POST /internal/issue-sponsor-token    → { tokenId, email, expiresAt }

### Admin (session + isAdmin required)
GET  /admin/stats               → { actCount, pendingJobs, userCount }
GET  /admin/jobs                → { jobs: [...] }
POST /admin/claim-act           → { ok, txId }           [CSRF]

GET  /admin/sponsor-tokens      → { tokens: [...] }
POST /admin/sponsor-tokens      → { tokenId, emailSent, emailError? }  [CSRF]
  Body: { email, note?, expiresInDays?, sendEmail? }
  sendEmail=true sends an invite email even if user hasn't registered yet.
DELETE /admin/sponsor-tokens/:id → { ok: true }          [CSRF]

GET  /admin/admins              → { admins: [{ userId, name, provider, createdAt }] }
POST /admin/admins              → { userId, name, provider }  [CSRF]
  Body: { email } — user must already be registered
DELETE /admin/admins/:userId    → { ok: true }           [CSRF]
  Cannot demote yourself.

## Error Format

All errors: HTTP 4xx/5xx with body { "error": "error_code_string" }

Common codes:
  unauthorized              — no valid session
  admin_required            — endpoint requires isAdmin
  csrf_invalid              — missing or wrong x-csrf-token
  already_linked            — user already has a Hive account linked
  previously_had_account    — user used their free account already (sponsor token overrides)
  global_daily_limit        — daily free account quota exhausted (resets midnight UTC)
  ip_daily_limit            — per-IP daily quota exhausted
  no_acts                   — Snapie has no pre-claimed account tokens
  invalid_username          — format or on-chain conflict
  insufficient_rc           — HTTP 503; account has no Resource Credits; RC recharges over time
                              For posting ops: the Snapie service account is out of RC (operator issue)
                              For active ops: the user's own account is out of RC
  emancipation_required     — account value exceeded threshold; user must export keys
  not_custodial             — action requires custodial mode
  user_not_found            — no account with that email (admin: grant admin)
  cannot_demote_self        — admin tried to revoke their own admin access

## Rate Limits (per IP)
  /api/auth/*               30 req / 15 min
  /api/auth/email/resend    5 req / 1 hr
  /api/account/create       5 req / 1 hr
  /api/account/check-*      40 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/sign-message    30 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/broadcast       60 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/transfer        10 req / 1 min
  /api/emancipate/*         5 req / 1 hr

## Hive Operations Allowed via /hive/broadcast
vote, comment, delete_comment, custom_json, claim_reward_balance

Active-level ops (transfer, power-up, power-down, delegate):
  Custodial   → server signs and broadcasts
  Emancipated → returns { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp }

## Message Signing (/hive/sign-message)
Signs an arbitrary message (max 512 chars) with the user's Hive posting key.
Uses SHA-256 hash of the message + secp256k1 signing (dhive cryptoUtils.sha256 + PrivateKey.sign).
The resulting signature is compatible with hive-js / dhive verifySignature.

Typical flow (challenge-response):
  1. Your app generates a nonce/challenge (e.g. UUID or timestamp-based string)
  2. POST /api/hive/sign-message { message: challenge }
  3. Your app verifies: verifySignature(account, challenge, signature, onChainPostingPubKey)
  4. On success: grant access to the feature

Emancipated users return needsClientSigning=true — handle with:
  Keychain: window.hive_keychain.requestSignBuffer(account, message, 'Posting', cb)
  AIOha:    aioha.signMessage(message, KeyTypes.Posting)

## Cookies
  snapieauth_session   httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax  — JWT RS256
  snapieauth_csrf      Secure, SameSite=Lax (JS-readable) — CSRF token
